finding.contract_claim: getNodeLabel accesses node.children (line 18) and node.size/node.extension (line 21) on a value typed FsNode = FileNode | DirectoryNode, and each property exists on only one union member — accessing the wrong variant would yield undefined at runtime.
finding.observed_behavior: Each access is guarded by a discriminant check on node.type (line 17): the `directory` branch reads .children and the post-guard fall-through is narrowed to FileNode before reading .size/.extension.
finding.triage_reasoning: The code uses a discriminant union narrowing pattern. Inside `if (node.type === "directory")`, TypeScript narrows `node` to `DirectoryNode`, so `node.children` is safe. After the `if`-block the only remaining type is `FileNode`, so `node.size` and `node.extension` are safe. The evidence confirms this: line 18 is inside the directory branch, line 21 is after the guard with a clarifying comment. No latent bug exists; the discriminant check fully eliminates the incorrect-variant access.
